item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Me, a genderfluid person: Give me the stone! Now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what would happen if someone were to be Rule 63’d IRL, isn’t it?

item.title: Oh my...
item.content:encoded: Yup this one gets an automatic like from me. Although it's a simple idea on paper, gender identity and sexuality has always been a delicate topic, to such an extent that some things about it are considered taboo (or at least, used to be. For better or worse, the Overton Window is shifting in favor of openness and acceptance and away from tradition). Any article that has the potential to trigger a meaningful discussion wherever it's used, so long as it's well-written and unique otherwise, has my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: can't help but wonder how people with de la chapelle syndrome or swyer syndrome would be affected. people with de la chapelle are genetically "female", as in, they have XX chromosomes, but have both the primary and secondary sex characteristics of men (swyer syndrome being the opposite, where women have XY chromosomes but appear phenotypically "female"). would it have no effect other than just switching the chromosomes in the cells?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: give me the stone

item.title: Re: Potential Scientific and Self Defense uses
item.content:encoded: why cant i delete posts i didn't check to see if i was dumb

item.title: This was a good scp
item.content:encoded: i like the concept, that's pretty cool

item.title: Re: classification
item.content:encoded: No because if you guard it in a box nothing happens

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I crosslinked to this anomaly in my Pride Month article!

SCP-4175 - Friends Made, and the Void

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This sorta reminds of me of 12 oz Mouse. If you don't know what 12 oz Mouse is, It's an old Adult Swim show, You really gotta see it for yourself to know if it's good or bad . There is this character called Man/Woman who is able to swap gender by saying "Man Power Go" or "Woman Power On". I really like this show, But what do you think?

Also, "The Gender-Bender" would have been A better name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another great SCP I heard of from back in 2013-2014. Glad I finally have a account to pay my respects to this anomaly and article.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one hasn't aged well

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Class Safe doesn't mean "not dangerous", but "easy to contain". Afterall, if you put SCP-113 in a secure box and just forget about it, it won't cause harm.
On the other hand, Euclid means that if you again put it in a box and forget about it, you can't predict what will happen. That's why sentient SCPs are often Euclid - you can't easily predict what they'll do.
SCP-113 is a rock. It's predictable and it won't break free if you leave it alone. Therefore, it's safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me personally this wasn't terrifying at all, I'd happily use this thing if it were real.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure when this article was last edited but right now it definitely seems like a sex-switcher rather than a gender-switcher since it doesn't seem to have any effect on gender identity

item.title: Staff Post - Disciplinary - Closed
item.content:encoded:
generic_username, using slurs on the SCP Wiki is against the rules. It is extremely rude, crude, and unnacceptable. Do not do this again. Please consider this an official warning, and continuing to break rules will result in disciplinary escalation.

This is a CLOSED staff post. Please do not respond unless you are staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Snip, snip - Lily

Also, there is no "hidden meaning" here. Keep in mind this was originally written back in the creepypasta days, when most authors had no grasp of basic English conventions, much less how to successfully write a hidden plot point. The re-write was more or less a clean-up of the original, nothing new there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: still…. it can be left in a box and nothing will happen…. it's safe

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is… wasted potential? Infuriatingly vague? It's just that the article is so verbose without actually covering that much conceptual ground, while Moose has hinted at some sort of tremendous implication or hidden meaning that, frankly, just can't be found in the text itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my two greatest wishes

sex reassignment or horrible death

please give me either

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I'd love to know is how sex-linked disorders and traits are affected. If a male is colour-blind, is there any chance of him loosing this trait once his gender switches? If he has male pattern baldness, is there any chance he might regain his hair? Or on a more serious note, if he has Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy, a fatal neuromuscular disorder, is there any possibility that this SCP might change this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you know how much people will pay to touch this thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man do I reply fast.

Sure it's painful, but anyone with bad dysphoria will happily tell you that the worst pain in the world for a minute or two is better than years of the psychological agony of, well, bad dysphoria.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-113 did, obviously. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will forever be disappointed in the fact that this isn't called SRS: Sex Reassignment Stone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hence why my vote is neutral, I'm kinda stuck in nostalgia for this site, I remember when 682 and 076 were some of the most dangerous skips on the site, and now we have stuff such as When Day Breaks and whatnot, it's kinda over the top and I both love and dislike that.

But the writing has improved *so much*, it's insane, and I gotta give credit where credit's due.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think if you don't quite understand "the whole gender thing", it'd be really insightful to do research into modern scientific research, and explore what's going on and how we're learning (and even relearning) these sorts of things. It's a very interesting topic :)

I personally understand why one would like the original if you have a certain taste in how SCPs or creepypasta can be written, though I will say the original is far less clinical in its tone, so I don't really see that as a favor in its court.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ha-HAAAaaah!

The joke here, for people who don't know, is that Azurill in some Pokemon games have a 75% chance of being female, whereas Marill have a 50% chance. So some Azurill will switch genders upon evolving due to game coding RNG.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll admit, I'm not an expert on the whole "gender" thing, I think it's all silly, but I'm an old fart so what do I know?

I've read this rewrite over and over, and I've come to the conclusion that I like the old version more, possibly due to personal bias, possibly due to the different tone. I recognize the effort of the rewrite however, and due to the skill behind the edit I'm gonna be neutral for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this is what happens when some Azurill evolve.
Huh..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What killed my motivation to write about this was 1) fear of misrepresentation and of controversy, 2) I wanted moose's permission and moose didn't like my pitch, and 3) the narrative I wanted to tell wouldn't have jived with moose's headcanon and moose's headcanon is actually kind of awesome even though it doesn't leak into the document in the slightest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been trying to write one on and off for a year or two with Diogenes as the main character. But I'm also pretty surprised that no one else has beaten anyone to the punch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Speaking of which, I don't know why we haven't had any Tales about this yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who hurt you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While my social and political attitudes have changed quite a bit since my comment on the last page, my opinion that something like this requires greater narrative exploration remains. Downvote rescinded because, if this were my first time reading this today, I'd neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite agreed! it's a fantastic article, and the edits just make it so much better!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this article deserves an upvote, IMO. Just saying.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: This is a closed staff post. Do not reply if you are not staff.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned for excessive vehemence and slur usage.

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
StarRelecture, while you're free to dislike the article and downvote it accordingly, your comment is disturbingly homophobic and transphobic. The word 'f***ot" is absolutely inappropriate and totally out of line. Additionally, any attempts to edit the article beyond minor grammatical and mechanical fixes will be reverted and you will face disciplinary action.

This is a closed staff post. Do not reply if you are not staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned for excessive vehemence and slur usage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned for excessive vehemence and slur usage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Transmasculine (FtM) Demiboy here, and I really appreciate the attempt to write an accurate article while putting it in foundation-speak. It's clear you know what you're talking about with it, definitely enough to be likely aware of terms like assigned male, etc, which led me to assume that the language choices were intentional. Good to know that's what it is. It strikes a good balance of recognizing and working with the existence of nonbinary genders and various forms of intersex, while being written by someone who doesn't know what words to use for this.

I'd be interested to see if there's different effects on mid-transition binary trans people (someone on hormones but without surgery, or only having had bottom or top surgery, or some other combination).

Overall, really good to see a "gender-swapping" scp that doesn't make me want to yell about the existence of intersex and nonbinary people.
+1

(hope this doesn't count as a necropost, but wanted to reply to this in specific)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I first read this article before the rewrite, probably back in 2012 sometime, thought it was neat, briefly wished it was a real thing, and moved on. Then in 2015, I finally came out to myself as transgender. Then today, while clicking Random repeatedly, I stumbled on this again. The rewrite is phenomenal, and I'm so very glad to discover that someone like thedeadlymoose is an admin here.

Sincerely, thank you. This just about made my week.

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: This is is an official warning to not continue being a shithead.

item.title: Re: Potential Scientific and Self Defense uses
item.content:encoded:
Gonna be a c-c-combo breaker, and actually give a relevant reply. Zynja'd

Just because you disagree with someone's viewpoints or dislike them as a person doesn't mean you need to be an ass about it.

Also the term "Feminazi" is rather disrespectful. Comparing anyone to Nazis, unless they're actually Nazis, just serves to lessen what the Nazis did, and that is not something we should ever do. The feminist movement is important, and while there are assholes in it, it's overall only helped people. And even with those assholes, I wouldn't wish harm on them.

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded: We've got seven post variants of the same sentiment. Let's keep the discussion focused on the article from here on, and refrain from spamming please.

item.title: Potential Scientific and Self Defense uses
item.content:encoded: Can I use this as Feminazi repellent? That would be seriously useful, just a little sprits on Lacy Green and she's gone XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's still painful tho, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The chance of death is only there if you try it twice.

item.title: Good work
item.content:encoded:
I am a post-transgender, or rather mtf post-surgery. I have no offence in the terminology used in SCPS-113's description. Likely it would have been written by some tech or Doctor without much experience or transgender/intersex/Queer exposure. This does not include when it was written, an how many times the file has been updated by other writers to the document.

However, I am curious to what experiments were done, and the results were. Not to mention on the history of it being found. The document states indirectly that at least a few experiments were made. I also wondered on a few other questions on experiments could be done on it. DeadlyMouse perhaps you should write som of the interactions and experiments were, and SPC-113 will tell you/us more about it, rather than trying to get technical about it in discussion here. Here are a few I thought could be interesting (some brought up I believe in this discussion forum for it):

1. Psychological impact of subjects to non-transgendered person to a transgendered person? To an intersex? Etc.
2. The physiological effect of a post-operative sexual reassignment transgender?
3. The impact of multiple transformations to a subject?

I also agree with you that Scps-113 is Safe class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Speaking of XX vs XY: with the Y chromosome being considerably smaller than the X chromosome, where does the extra DNA come from? Is it just mirrored from the X or created, and if created, from where? Past users possibly? Also what about people with two full chromosomes, and an extra full or partial? XX full Y vs XX partial Y is a thing in the human genome, and has very different implications?

Back to my first questions, what if this thing takes the extra bits from each XX conversion, and then adds it to the Y of any XY? Furthermore what if this thing is out of the extra snippets of genetic coding from XX cuts, and then an XY uses it? What happens if two people touch it at the same time? What if conjoined twins touch it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been reading a handful of SCPs from time to time for a year or so

This is really the problem: It's clear that you haven't been reading many of the modern SCP's or even some of the better older SCPs. Series I may be our roots, but the tree has grown beyond them.

To be blunt, many Series I articles wouldn't survive today, mostly for the two reasons you've mentioned: some are too cartoonish to take seriously, while others read like creepypasta. Nonetheless, there's a lot of nostalgia associated with some of these, and others have a hefty stack of old votes keeping them afloat. There's an ongoing effort to update some of the salvageable ones that fall into low ratings that's slowly working its way through and fixing the problem children.

Really, though, the site grew far beyond its creepypasta roots years ago. Today's SCP Foundation would be most accurately described as a Speculative Fiction site, spanning and fusing movements and genres including but not limited to New Weird, Bizarro Fic, Slipstream, Magical Realism, Two Fisted Tales, Xenofiction, Neo-Noir, Absurdism,Punk Fiction, and yes, occasionally comedy and horror.

At our best, we drag Lit Fic kicking and screaming into a world of talking animals, monsters, cyborg catgirls, mad scientists, and time loops.

TL;DR:
if you've only read a handful of Series I articles, you ain't seen nothing yet.
